title: China's Coal Import Prospects Still Not Bullish But Rest of the World Will Easily Compensate
section_path: China's Coal Import Prospects Still Not Bullish But Rest of the World Will Easily Compensate
section_path_text: China's Coal Import Prospects Still Not Bullish But Rest of the World Will Easily Compensate
summary:
Main
By Jeffrey Landsberg
As we have highlighted to our clients in Commodore's recent Weekly Dry Bulk Reports, China's coal production in April totaled 362.8 million tons. This has marked a month-on-month drop of 13.9 million tons (-8%) but is up year-on-year by 40.6 million tons (13%). While not setting a record (as was previously seen in March, December, November, and October), China's coal production has nevertheless stayed quite strong.
